prd_coverage.tool_evidence: ls found 24 wideframes covering 8 route screens across desktop/tablet/mobile, and PRD evidence names the same 8 required screens: dashboard, board, approval_gates, document_viewer, trace_explorer, task_detail, search_results, and task_list

component_traceability.tool_evidence: grep found 597 data-ds-id occurrences across 48 wireframe files; component-map.json is valid and maps 21 flow screens with 63 selector references and 18 unique blocks, with sampled wireframes showing matching selectors such as workspace.shell.header, dashboard.panel.gaps, task-detail.header.summary, and trace.canvas.graph

storyboard_completeness.tool_evidence: storyboards.json is valid and contains 21 trajectories; PRD journey grep surfaces 21 journey entries across board, approval, dashboard, navigation, docs, trace, task detail, search, and task list flows

layout_compilability.tool_evidence: layout-rules.json parsed successfully; it defines desktop/tablet/mobile viewports, global shell rules, and complete screen rules for 8 screens with placement guidance and shared responsive rules

conflict_resolution.tool_evidence: prd-ds-conflicts.md documents 2 active conflicts and gives a resolution direction for each, plus a normalized create-plan interaction decision; however propagation is incomplete because sampled task_detail mobile saving state still says 'spinner next to edited control' while the conflict report requires skeletons or non-spinner progress affordances

wireframe_articulation.tool_evidence: sampled wideframes and tree wireframes show strong nesting and annotations, but mandatory mechanical flow check returned arrows=0 for all 3 user-flow files, triggering FLOW_NOT_CONNECTED and capping this pillar at 20% of max despite otherwise detailed artifacts

fix_queue.detail: Repair user-flow ASCII formatting so the mandatory arrow-count check detects connected transitions; all three files currently returned arrows=0, which blocks Gate A even though the narrative flow content is otherwise present

Remove spinner-centric saving language from task detail wireframes and replace it with the normalized non-spinner progress affordance; /Users/steve/duyhunghd6/gmind/docs/design/contracts/prd-04-webui-and-pm-workspace/wireframes/task_detail--mobile.wideframe.ascii.md still states '[spinner next to edited control]'

Expand component-map coverage beyond the current 18 unique blocks so more of the selector-rich wireframe surface is explicitly tied back to journey blocks, especially shared shell elements and secondary action regions
